item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The domain of the website this image originated from (circa 2008) has long since expired, and so there is no indication this image was released under CC 3.0. As such, the image is considered non-compliant and has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an interesting idea. I like it. But, what does Marshall Carter and Dark have to do with this? Don't they usually create things for rich people?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if somebody just stands in the shadow and looks toward the light source? They'd see something. An outline.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What MC&D would do with this guy:

wouldn't it be amazing and cool to have a slave that could do this stuff for your amusement?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One question: Why MC&D?

I have become slightly less naive than I was during some of my earlier encounters with MC&D articles (i.e. downvoting/no vote because "people wouldn't do that"; I realize I was being very naive and foolish and showing off the fact that I live under a rock and also didn't even understand the premise of the group of interest, I know, I take it all back), but this just confuses me. How would even MC&D profit from this person's existence? They're a marketing company, right? How were they marketing this person? This isn't me saying "people wouldn't do that", it's me saying "what could people possibly do that would make this worth marketing?"; I think that's valid. Isn't it?

Maybe part of my confusion stems from the fact that the bit about MC&D is just kind of thrown into this article at one moment and then never elaborated on or even mentioned again. It just mentions a "distribution center" without even any qualifiers for that title…What else was there? What was the place being used for? What were they doing to Semi-Invisible Guy? Basically, it feels like the label "MC&D" was tossed in there solely to give the article a more Foundation-y feel, which I'm pretty sure is bad form. Does that make sense?

Also, what does Semi-Invisbile Guy claim his name is? I know we refer to scips by number, but we also try to at least partially humanize humanoid scips by giving them a name…it adds to the creep factor and stuff (yes, I realize I just said that in the lamest way possible, I'm sorry, it's late). Give the person a background, is what I'm saying. Make them into a person. Then make their existence tragic, that's a typical second step…maybe he used to be normal but then some wackos [DATA EXPUNGED] him?

…Gah, never mind, I'm tired and probably rambling. Upvoted in any case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The picture isn't very good in my opinion, Considering its very obviously a guy taking a photo of his shadow, making it not really weird or different. Maybe a better picture? And seeing others comments, I agree, even in its current form the final test still doesn't show enough to be interesting and spike curiosity, try writing out the whole thing, and then expunge parts of it, or none at all(?).
But I do like the idea, and this does show potential, holding back my vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the article but some suggestion (not even much) as to what happened to the D-class in the conclusion would be good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the flower eyes were a result of the surface-changes-to-match effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with thedeadlymoose. The expungement in the test log at 00.07.19 is quite silly as the only thing that can be assumed is that the D-Class also moved his arm. If you are going to expunge something in the test log then why not try it after a slightly more complicated maneuver? Perhaps tell 1058 to forcibly hold the D-Class's arm back. I'm assuming 1058 is standing behind the D-Class so if the D-Class tried to copy the movement of 1058 while 1058 was restraining him something more interesting might happen.

As for the second expungement, the one at "Test Conclusion", I don't really see any reason for it to be there. It is quite obvious that 1058 can control humans through their shadows (unless I am totally daft, which may be the case). If you add in something similar to my first suggestion you can put in this obvious statement and then [REDACTED] how the victim dies or something similar.

Anyways just my opinion. I see a lot of potential but as is I shall have to abstain from voting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would have upvoted, but the expungement is just too stupid. Therefore, downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last test brings to mind a wet tearing noise

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is what I attempted to use the image to convey, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The effect doesn't effect other people or excessively large objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found this very difficult to comprehend, but upvoted by the end because of what that last test suggests. I assume that the picture is showing the grass conforming to the subject's appearance, with the dandelions in place of his eyes. Is that's what's being suggested?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few thoughts on the containment procedures. Is the requirement of one single light to ensure that the SCP is always casting a single shadow? What would happen if multiple light sources are used, or if all lights are turned off? It seems to be implied in the test log, as the test immediately jumps to using the one light (just for the duration of the test). Is 1 cm of sand enough - what if enough sand is pushed aside that the shadow starts affecting the floor?

Also some questions on the invisibility effect. If a D class grabbed onto the SCP, would they become invisible? If a rope was tied to a D class, then tied to the SCP, would that turn the D class invisible? If someone is invisible due to that effect, can they see the SCP? If the SCP punches its hand into a wall, does the wall (or the whole building) disappear?

I thought the last log was good enough, and implied what was happening well enough based on the results of the other two tests. Guessing that the SCP moving its arms would tear the skin off the D class or something, as I imagine the shadow only affects the surface.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: glad someone used that picture, i always liked it, but i hate that the flower eyes are neglected. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of those "oh god" pictures. I'm slightly disappointed the article isn't as creepy as the picture, but it's still very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The [DATA EXPUNGED] is stupid and doesn't tell me anything about the article itself. Honestly.

Other than that, meh, no vote from me. I can't bring myself to like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for three reasons: first, it's a humanoid SCP that isn't a self insert. Second, I love the idea behind this. Third, I love that picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad to see this picture being used. I always liked it. That being said, I'm kinda meh about this concept. It's not bad, but it's not catching me. No vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that would probably work better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its meh

[DATA EXPUNGED]. D-21894 survived for four hours after the experiment.

Uhm, that sounds like shit

How about "D-21894 expired several hours after the test's conclusion."

Upvoted, nevertheless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to popular demand, I've un-expunged some of the last test log.
I hope it doesn't ruin the article for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do something with the last test and you'll have another upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very clever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Subject: SCP-1058
Test Coordinator: Senior Researcher ██████
Testing Environment: Standard Foundation 5m x 5m x 5m testing cell. D-21894 instructed to stand in SCP-1058's shadow for the duration of the test.

[DATA EXPUNGED]

Test Conclusion: [DATA EXPUNGED]"

Neutral vote for now, but I'll upvote if you fill in the [DATA EXPUNGED]s with something that's actually interesting.
